design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling platformer stages, collect items, defeat bosses to progress through themed worlds
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision and timing against enemy patterns and environmental hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as different Smurf characters with unique abilities to overcome obstacles and reach Gargamel
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Character-based progression system where different Smurfs are required for specific stage types (Brainy for brain-themed levels, etc.); multiple stage variants (standard platforming, minecart, snowsled) within progression

progression.upgrade_system: Character unlock system - player progresses through stages to unlock different Smurf characters with distinct abilities
progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat bosses and complete stages to unlock new Smurf types for use in subsequent levels
replayability_hooks: Hidden level select accessible via debug mode
Different Smurf characters with unique mechanics
Inaccessible bonus stages ('Cicogne' and 'Riviere') present in ROM suggest content gating

world_building_techniques: Themed stage progression (implied world map with geographic variation)
Character roster tied to narrative (different Smurfs deployed for different challenges)
Final boss confrontation with Gargamel signals story climax

boss_design.philosophy: Progressive difficulty scaling with distinct boss encounters at stage intervals; boss-specific mechanics suggested by debug test (Language Test code makes first two bosses instant-die, while Snake and Gargamel require one hit)
notable_bosses: First boss (defeated instantly in Language Test mode)
Second boss (defeated instantly in Language Test mode)
Snake boss (requires one hit to defeat; can still damage player)
Gargamel boss (final antagonist; requires one hit to defeat; can still damage player)

danger_signals: Enemy patterns in minecart and snowsled variant stages
environmental_storytelling: Themed stage environments (Brainy Smurf levels have distinct visual identity with removable shadow layer)
World map structure suggesting geographical progression
Stage type variation (standard platforming, minecart cavern, snowsled mountain) implies locational narrative

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Smurfs (SNES)
